query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Denigrate', 'To criticize unfairly; to disparage', 'Over time, the community learned to denigrate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Denizen', 'To grant rights of citizenship to, to naturalize', 'The author hoped the new policy would denizen tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Denominate', 'To call, to name', 'Critics argued that the plan might denominate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Denomination', 'The act of naming or designating', 'The essay highlights the denomination of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Denominator', 'The expression written below the line in a fraction', 'Scholars noted a striking denominator between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Denote', 'To indicate, to mark', 'The author hoped the new policy would denote tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Denouement', 'The final part of a play, movie, or narrative', 'The essay highlights the denouement of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Denounce', 'To publicly declare something to be wrong or evil', 'Critics argued that the plan might denounce the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dentifrice', 'Toothpaste or any other substance for cleaning the teeth', 'Scholars noted a striking dentifrice between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Denude', 'To divest of all covering, to make bare or naked, to strip', 'Critics argued that the plan might denude the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Denunciation', 'Proclamation, announcement, a publishing', 'Her denunciation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depict', 'To represent or illustrate', '“The painting depicts a serene countryside scene.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deplete', 'To use up the supply of a resource', 'Over time, the community learned to deplete its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deplorable', 'Deserving strong condemnation, shockingly bad', 'What seemed deplorable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deplore', 'To bewail, to weep bitterly over, to feel sorrow for', 'Over time, the community learned to deplore its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deploy', 'To arrange (usually a military unit or units) for use', 'Over time, the community learned to deploy its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deponent', '(Greek Grammar) having passive form but an active meaning', 'What seemed deponent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depopulate', 'To reduce the population of a region', 'Over time, the community learned to depopulate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deport', 'To expel someone from a country', 'Over time, the community learned to deport its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deportment', 'A person''s behavior or manners', 'The essay highlights the deportment of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deposition', 'The process of giving sworn evidence', 'Her deposition became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depositor', 'A person who makes a deposit, especially of money in a bank', 'Her depositor became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depository', 'A place where something is deposited for safekeeping', 'Scholars noted a striking depository between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deprave', 'To make morally bad or evil', 'The author hoped the new policy would deprave tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depravity', 'Moral corruption', 'Scholars noted a striking depravity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deprecate', 'Express disapproval; consider unimportant', 'The author hoped the new policy would deprecate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depreciat', 'Losing value over time', '“The car’s value has been depreciating rapidly.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depreciate', 'To reduce in value over time', 'The author hoped the new policy would depreciate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depreciation', 'Reduction in value over time', 'Scholars noted a striking depreciation between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depredation', 'The act of preying upon or plundering', 'The essay highlights the depredation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depress', 'To press down on', 'The author hoped the new policy would depress tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deprivation', 'The lack or denial of something necessary', '“Sleep deprivation can affect concentration.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Depth', 'The vertical distance below a surface', 'The essay highlights the depth of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deracinate', 'To pull up by the roots, to uproot, to extirpate', 'Over time, the community learned to deracinate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Derelict', 'Abandoned, forsaken, dilapidated, neglected', 'Scholars noted a striking derelict between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dereliction', 'Willful neglect of one''s duty', 'Scholars noted a striking dereliction between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deride', 'To mock or ridicule', 'Critics argued that the plan might deride the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Derisible', 'Subject to or deserving of contemptuous ridicule', 'Over time, the community learned to derisible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Derision', 'Mockery, taunt', 'Scholars noted a striking derision between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Derivation', 'The source from which something is derived, origin', 'Her derivation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Derivative', 'Imitative of the work of someone else', 'What seemed derivative at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Derive', 'To deduce (a conclusion) by reasoning', 'Critics argued that the plan might derive the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dermatology', 'The medical study of skin', 'Scholars noted a striking dermatology between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Derogatory', 'Showing a degrading or disrespectful attitude', 'The passage presents a derogatory portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Derrick', 'A crane with a movable pivoted arm for moving heavy objects', 'The essay highlights the derrick of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Derring-do', 'Valiant deeds in desperate times', 'Her derring-do became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Descendant', 'Descending from a biological ancestor', 'The essay highlights the descendant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Descent', 'An action of moving downward, dropping, falling', 'Scholars noted a striking descent between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Descry', 'To see by looking carefully', 'Critics argued that the plan might descry the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Desecrate', 'To profane or violate the sacredness/sanctity of something', 'Critics argued that the plan might desecrate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
